2605.20644.title: Design for Manufacturing: A Manufacturability Knowledge-Integrated Reinforcement Learning Framework for Free-Form Pipe Routing in Aeroengines
2605.20644.paper_summary: Design for manufacturing plays a critical role in advanced aeroengine development, where complex components necessitate careful consideration of manufacturability. However, current practices in pipe routing remain largely decoupled from down-stream manufacturing, leading to labor-intensive, trial-and-error iterations to achieve manufacturable designs. To address this problem, this study proposes the Frenet-based pipe routing optimization (FPRO) framework, a manufacturability knowledge-integrated reinforcement learning approach for free-form pipe design in aeroengines. FPRO formulates the routing problem as a boundary value problem in the Frenet frame. In this framework, the pipe path is represented by curvature and torsion profiles, which are generated using cubic Hermite interpolation. To integrate design and manufacturing, domain-specific manufacturing knowledge is embedded as constraints on the permissible ranges of curvature and torsion. The path optimization is performed using the proximal policy optimization algorithm with stochastic exploration and a stage-guided reward mechanism. A unified mapping formulation then translates the optimized path into motion trajectories for the bending die, enabling direct fabrication on a six-axis free-bending machine. Experimental results demonstrate that FPRO consistently generates collision-free, manufacturable paths with smoother geometric profiles compared to Cartesian-based methods. It also achieves faster convergence and superior performance in terminal alignment, path length, obstacle avoidance, and manufacturability compared to state-of-the-art reinforcement learning baselines. Real-world validation confirms the close geometric correspondence between the manufactured pipe and its digital design, validating the practical feasibility of FPRO.
2605.20644.paper_summary_zh: 製造設計在先進航空發動機開發中扮演著關鍵角色，複雜的組件需要仔細考慮可製造性。<lb/>然而，目前的管道路由實踐在很大程度上與下游製造脫節，導致需要勞動密集型的試錯迭代來實現可製造的設計。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，本研究提出了基於Frenet的管道路由優化（FPRO）框架，這是一種將可製造性知識整合的強化學習方法，用於航空發動機中的自由形狀管道設計。<lb/>FPRO將路由問題表述為Frenet框架中的邊界值問題。<lb/>在這個框架中，管道路徑由曲率和扭轉剖面表示，這些剖面是使用三次Hermite插值生成的。<lb/>為了整合設計和製造，特定領域的製造知識被嵌入作為曲率和扭轉的允許範圍約束。<lb/>路徑優化是使用帶有隨機探索的近端策略優化算法和階段引導獎勵機制進行的。<lb/>然後，統一的映射公式將優化的路徑轉換為彎曲模具的運動軌跡，使得在六軸自由彎曲機上能夠直接製造。<lb/>實驗結果表明，FPRO與基於笛卡爾的方法相比，能夠持續生成無碰撞、可製造的路徑，且幾何剖面更平滑。<lb/>與最先進的強化學習基準相比，它在終端對齊、路徑長度、避障和可製造性方面也實現了更快的收斂和更優的性能。<lb/>現實世界的驗證確認了製造的管道與其數字設計之間的幾何對應性，驗證了FPRO的實際可行性。
2605.20644.author: Caicheng Wang et.al.
2605.20644.authors: Caicheng Wang, Zili Wang, Shuyou Zhang, Yongzhe Xiang, Zheyi Li, Liangyou Li, Jianrong Tan
